display_name: Port configuration change to ASIC (speed/MTU/admin state)

steps.action: For speed and MTU: 'config interface speed/mtu' delegates to the portconfig script which calls ConfigDBConnector.mod_entry() on the PORT table. For admin state: 'config interface startup/shutdown' calls config_db.mod_entry() directly in config/main.py. All paths write the changed field (speed, mtu, or admin_status) to CONFIG_DB PORT table.

PortMgr::doTask() receives CONFIG_DB PORT table SET event via ConsumerStateTable. For already-created ports (portOk=true), it applies kernel-side settings immediately: setPortMtu() runs 'ip link set dev <ifname> mtu <mtu>' and setPortAdminStatus() runs 'ip link set dev <ifname> up|down'. After the kernel call succeeds, writeConfigToAppDb() publishes the updated field to APPL_DB PORT_TABLE. Speed changes are NOT handled by portmgrd — speed is passed through to APPL_DB directly without a kernel ip-link call (kernel netdev speed is opaque; ASIC speed is set by orchagent).

PortsOrch::doPortTask() receives APPL_DB PORT_TABLE SET event via ConsumerStateTable. For an existing port (portExists=true) it parses the updated fields via parsePortConfig(). For speed changes it calls setPortSpeed() which invokes sai_port_api->set_port_attribute(SAI_PORT_ATTR_SPEED). For MTU it calls setPortMtu() which adds Ethernet/VLAN overhead (22 bytes) and calls sai_port_api->set_port_attribute(SAI_PORT_ATTR_MTU). For admin state it calls setPortAdminStatus() which calls sai_port_api->set_port_attribute(SAI_PORT_ATTR_ADMIN_STATE). The sairedis client stub serializes each SAI call and writes it to ASIC_DB as a SET command on ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_PORT.

Syncd::processSingleEvent() reads the SET command from ASIC_DB ASIC_STATE channel. It dispatches to processQuadEvent(SAI_COMMON_API_SET, ...) which calls processOidSet(). processOidSet() translates the virtual object ID (VID) to a real hardware RID and calls m_vendorSai->set(SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_PORT, rid, attr) — the vendor SAI library. The vendor SAI programs the speed, MTU, or admin state directly into the ASIC forwarding hardware.
steps.output: Port speed, MTU, or admin state programmed in ASIC hardware

steps.actor: syncd (NotificationProcessor) — oper status feedback, SAI path
steps.action: When the ASIC detects a link state change (e.g. admin down causes link down, or speed renegotiation completes), the vendor SAI fires the on_port_state_change callback. NotificationProcessor::process_on_port_state_change() translates the hardware RID to a VID and calls sendNotification(SAI_SWITCH_NOTIFICATION_NAME_PORT_STATE_CHANGE, ...) which publishes the notification to ASIC_DB NOTIFICATIONS channel.

steps.actor: orchagent (PortsOrch) — oper status feedback, SAI path
steps.action: PortsOrch::handleNotification() receives the port_state_change notification from ASIC_DB NOTIFICATIONS channel via m_portStatusNotificationConsumer. It deserializes the payload with sai_deserialize_port_oper_status_ntf(), looks up the port object, and calls updatePortOperStatus(). updatePortOperStatus() calls updateDbPortOperStatus() which writes the new oper_status ('up' or 'down') back to APPL_DB PORT_TABLE so that all consumers (intfsyncd, neighsyncd, etc.) can react.

steps.actor: portsyncd (LinkSync) — oper status feedback, netlink path
steps.action: In parallel with the SAI notification path, the kernel raises RTM_NEWLINK when the host interface changes state (IFF_RUNNING flips). portsyncd's LinkSync::onMsg() receives the netlink message, reads the admin and oper flags plus the current MTU from the link object, and writes state='ok', netdev_oper_status='up|down', admin_status, and mtu to STATE_DB PORT_TABLE. This is the authoritative source for the kernel-visible interface state that portmgrd and other daemons use to gate config application.

failure_injection_points.failure: portmgrd not running or not subscribed to CONFIG_DB PORT table
failure_injection_points.symptom: CONFIG_DB PORT shows updated speed/mtu/admin_status but APPL_DB PORT_TABLE is not updated; 'ip link show Ethernet0' still shows old MTU or admin state
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec swss supervisorctl status portmgrd; check /var/log/swss/portmgrd.log; verify portmgrd is consuming CONFIG_DB: redis-cli -n 4 MONITOR | grep PORT

failure_injection_points.failure: orchagent not processing APPL_DB PORT_TABLE update
failure_injection_points.symptom: APPL_DB PORT_TABLE shows updated fields but ASIC_DB ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_PORT does not reflect new speed/MTU/admin state; port hardware state unchanged
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent; check /var/log/swss/orchagent.log for 'setPortSpeed' or 'setPortMtu' errors; redis-cli -n 0 HGETALL 'PORT_TABLE:Ethernet0' to confirm APPL_DB write

failure_injection_points.failure: syncd SAI call failing for port attribute set
failure_injection_points.symptom: ASIC_DB ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_PORT shows updated attribute but port hardware not programmed; orchagent log shows SAI_STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED or SAI_STATUS_FAILURE
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec syncd supervisorctl status syncd; check /var/log/syncd.log for SAI error codes on SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_PORT; verify speed is in supported_speeds: redis-cli -n 6 HGET 'PORT_TABLE|Ethernet0' supported_speeds

failure_injection_points.failure: No oper status notification from vendor SAI after admin-up or speed change
failure_injection_points.symptom: Port programmed in ASIC (syncd no errors) but APPL_DB oper_status stays 'down'; 'show interfaces status' shows port down even though cable is connected
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check if SAI notification callback is registered: grep 'on_port_state_change' /var/log/syncd.log; check physical layer — transceiver present? show interfaces transceiver presence; verify ASIC_DB NOTIFICATIONS: redis-cli -n 1 SUBSCRIBE NOTIFICATIONS

failure_injection_points.failure: orchagent not receiving or processing port_state_change notification
failure_injection_points.symptom: ASIC_DB NOTIFICATIONS shows port_state_change events but APPL_DB PORT_TABLE oper_status is stale; protocols using oper status (BGP, etc.) see wrong state
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check orchagent NotificationConsumer: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent; look for 'port_state_change' in /var/log/swss/orchagent.log; redis-cli -n 0 HGET 'PORT_TABLE:Ethernet0' oper_status

failure_injection_points.failure: portsyncd not receiving RTM_NEWLINK netlink events
failure_injection_points.symptom: STATE_DB PORT_TABLE|Ethernet0 missing state='ok' or shows stale netdev_oper_status; portmgrd cannot apply further config changes because isPortStateOk() returns false
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec swss supervisorctl status portsyncd; check /var/log/swss/portsyncd.log; ip link show Ethernet0 to verify kernel interface state; portmgrd will queue config changes until STATE_DB state='ok' is set
